cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out what date is one week from the last day of 1899. First, I need to confirm what the last day of 1899 is. Well, the last day of any year is December 31st of that year. So the starting date is December 31, 1899. Now, I need to add one week, which is 7 days, to this date. Adding 7 days to December 31, 1899, we can just count forward day by day. Let me start counting the days: - December 31, 1899 + 1 day = January 1, 1900 (New Year's Day) - January 1, 1900 + 1 day = January 2, 1900 - January 2, 1900 + 1 day = January 3, 1900 - January 3, 1900 + 1 day = January 4, 1900 - January 4, 1900 + 1 day = January 5, 1900 - January 5, 1900 + 1 day = January 6, 1900 - January 6, 1900 + 1 day = January 7, 1900 So, adding 7 days to December 31, 1899, we arrive at January 7, 1900. Let me check if the year 1900 is correct. Yes, because December has 31 days, so December 31 is the last day, and adding one day takes us into January of the next year, which would be 1900. Now let me check the options given. The format is MM/DD/YYYY, so I need to format my answer accordingly. January is the first month, so it's 01. The day is 07, so it's 07. The year is 1900. So the date would be 01/07/1900. Looking at the options provided: (A) 02/07/1900 (B) 02/22/1900 (C) 01/13/1900 (D) 01/07/1900 (E) 10/07/1900 (F) 01/07/1828 The correct option is (D) 01/07/1900.
